front: Novel: picaresque tale whose title character is described as 'an American, Chicago born'
back: The Adventures of Augie March (Saul Bellow)

front: Novel: the protagonist works for Einhorn and catches eagles with Thea Fenchel before surviving a shipwreck on a lifeboat with a madman
back: The Adventures of Augie March (Saul Bellow)

front: Novel: in Mexico, the title character finds his friend Sylvester has become a bodyguard for Trotsky
back: The Adventures of Augie March (Saul Bellow)

front: Novel: a failed actor and salesman is conned into speculating on lard commodities by the sinister Dr. Tamkin
Novel: the protagonist's father Dr. Adler refuses to give him money; he ends up weeping at a stranger's funeral
Novel: the protagonist works as an extra playing a musician but is embarrassed when no sound comes from his fake bagpipes
Novel: a divorced Jewish professor writes unsent letters to celebrities while processing his divorce from Madeleine and betrayal by Valentine Gerbasch
Novel: the scholar Shapiro admits 'my Russian is not what it could be' despite giving long pronouncements on Dostoevsky
Novel: based on friendship with Delmore Schwartz; the protagonist Charlie Citrine befriends the fictionalized poet Von Humboldt Fleisher
Novel: an American millionaire tries to remove frogs from the Arnewi cistern and lifts a statue of the goddess Mummah to become Rain King of the Wariri
Novel: King Dahfu hunts a lion he believes is the reincarnation of his dead father; Romilayu serves as the protagonist's guide in Africa
Novel: a fictionalization of Allan Bloom as a political philosopher who dies of AIDS at the Hotel Crillon in Paris
Novel: inspired partly by its author nearly dying from ciguatera poisoning after eating a toxic fish
Author: dying words were reportedly 'Was I a man or was I a jerk?'
Author: caused controversy by asking 'Who is the Tolstoy of the Zulus?'; defended himself by citing Thomas Mofolo's Chaka
Author: translated Isaac Bashevis Singer's 'Gimpel the Fool' from Yiddish, introducing Singer to English-speaking readers
Author: wrote the introduction to Allan Bloom's The Closing of the American Mind
